explanation: This command simulates a PowerShell reflective assembly load using base64 encoded data. It is a direct representation of the Sigma rule's detection pattern.
This command uses the short form of the -Command parameter (-c) and fully qualifies the Convert class, which is a syntactic variation of the original.
This command wraps the PowerShell command in a script block using &, which is a valid PowerShell syntax variation.
This command adds an optional parameter (-WindowStyle Hidden) that does not affect the core behavior of the reflective load but is often used for evasion.
This command uses backticks to escape the quotes around the base64 string, which is a valid PowerShell syntax variation.
This command uses double backslashes to escape the quotes around the base64 string, which is a valid PowerShell syntax variation.
This command adds the -ExecutionPolicy Bypass parameter, which is often used to allow script execution but does not alter the core reflective load behavior.
This command adds the -NonInteractive parameter, which is often used in automation but does not affect the core reflective load behavior.
This command adds the -NoProfile parameter, which is often used to speed up execution but does not affect the core reflective load behavior.
This command appends an empty command string, which is a harmless addition that does not affect the core reflective load behavior.
